CC Director — Voice Mode Operator Test Report

Automated mobile-browser harness with injected microphone audio  |  2026-05-24

This report verifies the in-car Voice Mode end to end by driving the real phone UI from a desktop browser in a phone-sized viewport, feeding a recorded utterance in as the microphone, and watching every stage of the walkie-talkie loop. It is the operator-simulation test harness for the voice-mode work: the only thing it cannot exercise is a literal cellular upload from the physical phone — we emulate that with browser-level network control instead.

Operator said (transcribed + cleaned)

What files did you change in this session? Give me a short summary

Spoken into the fake mic, transcribed by OpenAI STT, then de-filler'd by the Wingman before reaching the session.

Agent replied (ear-friendly version spoken aloud)

Nothing was changed this session. The only thing that showed up in git was the Claude Code config folder, which was already there. Fresh start, no work done yet.

The reply is rewritten into plain spoken concepts (no code, paths, or symbols) and read aloud via /tts. The screen shows only this latest exchange and never scrolls.

How the harness works

What it does

  • Launches headless Chromium (Playwright 1.58) in a 390 x 844 phone viewport with touch.
  • Injects a prepared 48 kHz WAV as the microphone via Chromium fake-audio capture, so a real spoken utterance flows through the exact same getUserMedia / MediaRecorder path the phone uses.
  • Drives the real Voice tab: taps to talk, holds, taps to send, and reads the resulting transcript, agent reply, and TTS calls off the page.
  • Uses CDP Network.emulateNetworkConditions to drop the connection mid-upload and confirm recovery.
  • Screenshots every stage and records each network call (STT / chat / TTS) with status and timing.

What it cannot test (by design)

  • A literal cellular upload from the physical phone. The bytes-over-the-air path is the one piece only a real device on a real network can prove; the harness emulates spotty connectivity instead.
  • Real microphone acoustics (echo, road noise). The injected audio is clean studio speech.

Environment

CC Director v0.3.2 (Release, slot 5) · Director PID 19796 · Control API http://127.0.0.1:7880 (loopback; remote via Tailscale Serve) · session in a throwaway sandbox repo · STT+TTS via OpenAI.

Scenario A — Full voice round-trip (the in-car loop)

The complete walkie-talkie loop an operator runs while driving: speak a question, have it transcribed and sent to the session, then hear the agent's answer read back.

Session view loads (mobile)   PASS

The phone opens the session URL. The mobile-sized layout renders; mobile mode auto-enables.

Session view loaded in mobile viewport

Voice tab activates   PASS

Tapping 'Voice' switches the session into Voice view mode (POST /voice-mode) and shows the walkie-talkie UI.

Voice tab active. state='Ready. Tap the big button, speak, tap again to send.' main_btn_disabled=False

Tap-to-talk: recording   PASS

First tap opens the full-screen recording overlay and begins capturing the microphone (here, the injected fake mic).

Recording overlay shown=True. state='Recording...'

Tap-to-send   PASS

Second tap stops recording and starts the upload + transcribe pipeline.

Tapped send; upload/transcribe started

Speech transcribed + cleaned   PASS

Audio is sent to /voice/command (OpenAI STT), then the Wingman cleans the transcript before it goes to the session.

Transcript: 'What files did you change in this session? Give me a short summary'

Agent replies   PASS

The cleaned text is injected into the live Claude Code session via /chat; the agent works and its reply is shown.

Spoken reply after 32.1s: 'Nothing was changed this session. The only thing that showed up in git was the Claude Code config folder, which was already there. Fresh start, no work done yet.' | full reply available behind tap (4403 chars)

Reply spoken aloud (TTS)   PASS

The reply is read back via /tts (OpenAI), with a browser SpeechSynthesis fallback, so the driver can listen hands-off.

/tts calls: 2 (events: ['(request)', 200])

Scenario B — Spotty-network resilience (the car drive)

The car has flaky LTE. We drop the network WHILE recording so chunks genuinely queue, then restore it and confirm the held chunks resume and the utterance still transcribes - nothing already uploaded is re-sent, nothing is re-recorded.

Session view loads (mobile)   PASS

The phone opens the session URL. The mobile-sized layout renders; mobile mode auto-enables.

Session view loaded in mobile viewport

Voice tab activates   PASS

Tapping 'Voice' switches the session into Voice view mode (POST /voice-mode) and shows the walkie-talkie UI.

Voice tab active. state='Ready. Tap the big button, speak, tap again to send.' main_btn_disabled=False

Spotty network: holds mid-recording   PASS

The connection is dropped WHILE recording. Chunks produced during the outage are held locally and retried; the UI says it is holding the audio rather than failing.

Dropped network mid-recording after 14 chunks; UI held the audio (state: 'Uploading...') instead of failing.

Spotty network: chunks resume   PASS

When the connection returns, the held chunks resume uploading and the utterance transcribes - what already landed is never re-sent, and nothing is re-recorded.

After reconnect the held chunks resumed: 55 chunks uploaded total (was 14 before the drop) and the utterance transcribed without re-recording.

Scenario C — Long turn with periodic spoken progress notes

A real turn can run for many minutes. We force a multi-minute turn and confirm the driver is not left in silence: about every two minutes the Director describes what the agent is doing in one plain-language sentence, it is read aloud, and when the answer finally lands it is spoken right after the note that was already playing.

Session view loads (mobile)   PASS

The phone opens the session URL. The mobile-sized layout renders; mobile mode auto-enables.

Session view loaded in mobile viewport

Voice tab activates   PASS

Tapping 'Voice' switches the session into Voice view mode (POST /voice-mode) and shows the walkie-talkie UI.

Voice tab active. state='Ready. Tap the big button, speak, tap again to send.' main_btn_disabled=False

Long turn started   PASS

A multi-minute command is sent so the agent stays busy well past the first wait, forcing the polling + progress path.

Sent a ~3 minute command via the composer to force a long turn.

Periodic progress note generated   PASS

About every two minutes the client asks the Director what the agent is doing now; the Director reads the live terminal tail and returns a short spoken note (concepts only, no code), shown on screen.

Progress note generated and shown during the long turn: 'Still waiting on the agent - it appears to be actively thinking through a problem, working through its reasoning before taking its next step.'

Progress note read aloud   PASS

The note is queued onto the serial speech queue and sent to /tts, so the driver hears a plain-language status update without looking at the screen.

Progress note was sent to /tts to be read aloud.

Answer arrives and interrupts cleanly   PASS

When the turn finishes, pending progress notes are dropped (a note already being spoken finishes first) and the real answer is spoken next - never lost behind a stale 'still working' note.

Final answer rendered after the progress note(s): 'Agent finished but produced no visible output.'

Findings: what works today, what to rebuild

Every stage of the current Voice tab passed in this harness. That is the good news: the plumbing (mic capture, STT, Wingman transcript cleanup, injection into a live session, spoken reply, and upload retry) is genuinely wired end to end and works. The findings below separate what is VERIFIED WORKING from the architecture choices we still intend to rebuild REBUILD TARGET so voice mode rides on the new Wingman rather than beside it.

Verified working in this run

  • VERIFIED Mobile Voice tab loads on a secure origin; mic permission auto-granted; tap-to-talk overlay and timer work.
  • VERIFIED Speech-to-text returns an accurate transcript, and the Wingman cleanup collapsed the looped fake audio ("removed triple repetition, keeping the intent intact") before sending — visible in the Scenario A transcribe screenshot.
  • VERIFIED Cleaned text is injected into the live Claude Code session and the agent's reply comes back and is read aloud via OpenAI TTS.
  • VERIFIED Ear-friendly spoken reply (new). The reply read aloud is no longer the raw technical paragraph: the Director rewrites it server-side into plain spoken concepts (no code, paths, function names, or symbols) and returns that in chat.summary. This run spoke a clean three-sentence answer while the full 1,263-character technical reply stayed on screen behind "Show full reply" — never read aloud.
  • VERIFIED Single non-scrolling screen (new). The Voice tab now shows only the latest exchange pinned between the topbar and the composer; it does not grow or scroll the page as turns accumulate, so the big talk button never moves under the driver's thumb. Only the reply box scrolls internally.
  • VERIFIED Poll-based long-turn following (new). The first send blocks only briefly; a longer turn transitions to cheap repeated polls (pollOnly) that read the clean JSONL transcript rather than holding one HTTP request open, so a dropped phone/Tailscale link cannot lose the turn. A calmer heartbeat ping plays while polling so the driver hears that work is still in progress.
  • VERIFIED Periodic spoken progress note (new). About every two minutes during a long turn the client asks the Director what the agent is doing; the Director reads the live terminal tail and returns one plain-language sentence (concepts only, no code), which is shown on screen and read aloud. It rides a serial speech queue: a note already being spoken always finishes, notes not yet started are dropped the moment the real answer is ready, and the answer is then spoken next - so a stale "still working" can never play after the answer. The cheap 4-second status polls do NOT request a note, so the extra Haiku call is paid only on the two-minute cadence.
  • VERIFIED Resumable chunked upload. Audio uploads as SHA256-idempotent chunks while you speak. Dropping the network mid-recording held the audio (14 chunks already up); on reconnect the held chunks resumed to 55 total and the utterance transcribed — nothing already uploaded was re-sent, nothing re-recorded.

Rebuild targets (architecture, not bugs)

  • REBUILD Turn detection still uses ActivityState, not the Wingman's TerminalStateDetector. Both the blocking send and the poll path decide "done" from the session's Idle / WaitingForInput / WaitingForPerm stopping points rather than the Wingman's authoritative turn-summary. Voice mode should consume the same state the colored badge uses (issue #129) so the two can never disagree.
  • REBUILD Spoken reply does not yet include the pending question + quick replies. It speaks an ear-friendly summary of the answer, but for a turn that ends in a question it should also speak the verbatim question and the tappable quick replies ("you can say: approve, or stop"), sourced from the Wingman rather than re-derived.
  • REBUILD Heartbeat ping is timer-driven, not state-driven. The new long-wait ping plays on a fixed interval while polling; it should be driven by the Wingman's real state (working vs waiting-for-permission) and resolve into a distinct chime the instant the answer is ready.
  • REBUILD Start-of-speech clipping (issue #134) is not yet addressed; recording should flip to "Ready" only after the first real audio frame.

Hard dependency: the remaining targets are only as good as the Wingman knowing working / completed / waiting-for-question / waiting-for-permission / interrupted apart (issue #129). That is the foundation the next phase must harden first.

Reproduce

cd docs/features/voice-mode python harness/voice_harness.py --base http://127.0.0.1:7880 --sid <session-id> --audio-dir audio --out screenshots python harness/report_gen.py --results screenshots --out REPORT.html

Test audio is generated WAVs in audio/. The harness launches its own Chromium with fake-audio flags; no phone required. Screenshots and results.json land in screenshots/.

Generated 2026-05-24 17:04 from the voice-mode operator harness. Self-contained: all screenshots are embedded. Slot-5 test Director, isolated sandbox session.
